config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak dasar justru melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari keseimbangan: kepentingan umum dijaga, tetapi martabat dan kebebasan pribadi tetap dihormati. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama “masyarakat”, itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any rule that lets “the common good” become a blank check for government or majorities to trample speech, due process, privacy, or bodily autonomy. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety and basic order matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. To me, a free society is strongest when it protects people from being sacrificed for vague collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dijaga adalah keseimbangan: kepentingan publik penting, tetapi hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil tidak boleh mudah dikorbankan. Saya juga setuju bahwa hak individu memang tidak absolut, misalnya saat menyangkut keselamatan umum, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar ketat, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” hanya jadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau tidak punya suara.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am especially skeptical of any argument that lets the majority or the state override speech, privacy, due process, or bodily autonomy in the name of a vague public good. The other side is right that society does need some limits for safety and order, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable, not a default rule that puts individuals second. In the United States, history has shown that once “the common good” becomes an excuse to weaken rights, the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk hal yang perlu, tapi juga terlalu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk membungkam kritik, membatasi kebebasan, atau menekan kelompok yang lemah. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan aturan default. Masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika kepentingan bersama dijaga tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am very skeptical of any broad claim that “the common good” should outrank rights, because in the United States that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, surveillance, discrimination, or other abuses against unpopular people. Society does need limits in cases like public safety and disease control, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to real oversight rather than treated as a default. To me, a society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only restricts them when it can clearly prove that doing so is necessary and fair.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
